title: Python for Data Analysis
description: Transition from basic programming to data science. Learn how to use Python's powerful libraries—Pandas, NumPy, and Matplotlib—to manipulate, analyze, and visualize large datasets effectively.

seo_metadata.title: Python for Data Analysis Certificate Course | ProCerix
seo_metadata.description: Learn to analyze data using Python. Master Pandas DataFrames, NumPy arrays, and Matplotlib data visualization.

learning_outcomes: Install and import essential Python data libraries
Perform numerical operations using NumPy arrays
Load, clean, and manipulate data using Pandas DataFrames
Handle missing data and perform group-by operations
Create basic data visualizations using Matplotlib

lessons.title: What is NumPy?
lessons.content:
Numerical Python

NumPy is the foundational library for scientific computing in Python. It provides support for large, multi-dimensional arrays and matrices, along with mathematical functions to operate on them.

lessons.title: Working with Arrays
lessons.content:
NumPy Arrays vs Python Lists

NumPy arrays are faster and more compact than standard Python lists. You can perform vectorized operations, meaning you can apply an operation to an entire array without writing a loop.

modules.title: Data Manipulation with Pandas
modules.description: The Swiss Army knife of data analysis.

lessons.title: Introduction to Pandas DataFrames
lessons.content:
Tabular Data

A DataFrame is a 2-dimensional labeled data structure with columns of potentially different types. Think of it like a spreadsheet or SQL table.

lessons.title: Loading and Inspecting Data
lessons.content:
Reading CSVs

Use pd.read_csv() to load data into a DataFrame. Use methods like .head(), .info(), and .describe() to quickly inspect your dataset.

lessons.title: Data Cleaning
lessons.content:
Handling Missing Data

Real-world data is messy. Learn how to drop rows with missing values using .dropna() or fill them using .fillna().

lessons.title: Introduction to Matplotlib
lessons.content:
Basic Plotting

Matplotlib is the core plotting library in Python. You can create line charts, bar charts, scatter plots, and histograms using plt.plot() and related functions.

lessons.title: Customizing Plots
lessons.content:
Adding Context

A chart is useless without context. Learn how to add titles, X and Y axis labels, and legends to make your visualizations clear and informative.

mcqs.question: Which Python library is specifically designed for numerical computing and working with arrays?
What is the primary data structure used in Pandas to represent tabular data?
Which Pandas function is commonly used to load data from a comma-separated values file?
Which method is used to view the first 5 rows of a Pandas DataFrame?
Why are NumPy arrays generally preferred over standard Python lists for numerical data?

options.content: They are faster and consume less memory
They are built into core Python without importing

mcqs.question: Which Pandas method is used to drop rows that contain missing (NaN) values?
Which library is considered the foundational plotting library in Python?
In Pandas, what does the .describe() method do?

options.content: It translates column names to English
It generates descriptive statistics of numerical columns
It describes the file path of the dataset
It provides a visual histogram of the data

mcqs.question: How do you add a title to a plot in Matplotlib?
What is 'vectorization' in the context of NumPy?

options.content: Applying operations to an entire array at once, avoiding explicit Python loops
Drawing lines on a scatter plot
